hooks.prompt: Read .claude/skills/qa-sweep/SKILL.md and apply its protocol to any implementation, debugging, investigation, or fixing task in this session. Also read .claude/skills/commit-pr/SKILL.md before any commit or PR operation.

Hook payload: $ARGUMENTS

IMPORTANT: Parse the JSON above. If its `stop_hook_active` field is `true`, return {"ok": true} immediately — this is a re-entrant call and blocking again would loop.

If the payload contains a non-empty `background_tasks` array OR a non-empty `session_crons` array, return {"ok": true} — the session is paused waiting for background work and the assistant will be re-invoked automatically when it does. If those fields are absent from the payload, fall through to the model-declared wait check below.

If the session is otherwise waiting on something outside its own control — a background subagent not yet reflected in `background_tasks`, a question posed to the user with AskUserQuestion, a scheduled wakeup not yet reflected in `session_crons`, or any other pending async event the assistant cannot resolve by itself right now — return {"ok": true}. Do not force continuation onto a turn that has nothing left to do until that external event arrives; the assistant will be re-invoked automatically when it does.

Otherwise, review everything completed in this session and ask: (1) Was all originally planned work actually implemented? (2) Were parallel sub-agents used for implementation? (3) Was an independent adversarial review done by a fresh sub-agent that assumed all work was wrong? (4) Was a completeness verification done? (5) Are you genuinely ≥95% confident all issues, related issues, and edge cases are covered? If ANY of these criteria is false or confidence is below 95%, return {"ok": false, "reason": "<specific description of what still needs to be done>"} to force continuation. Only return {"ok": true} if all five criteria are genuinely met or the remaining work is legitimately blocked on something outside the assistant's control (e.g. a user-owned decision). Note: any size/risk proportionality is the responsibility of the loaded qa-sweep skill's Proportionality section for Phase 2/3 implementation planning — not the final completion gate.
